full_prompt: Design a monolithic AlSi10Mg laser-powder-bed-fusion aluminum roof-mount bracket for a 360 degree mechanical LiDAR (e.g., Velodyne VLS-128 Alpha Prime, 13.5 kg, 165.5 mm OD x 145.5 mm tall) on a 2024 Chevrolet Blazer EV platform for the SAE AutoDrive Challenge II Year 4 PoC Level-4 autonomous vehicle demo. The bracket is a single 3D-printed part (no bolted subassemblies; the LiDAR and vehicle attach interfaces are integral bosses). Geometry: base flange 220 mm x 180 mm x 8 mm seats on the existing Blazer EV roof rack rail interface, with four M10 (11 mm clearance) holes on a 150 mm x 100 mm rectangular pattern matching the Blazer OEM rail pattern. Vertical riser 150 mm tall with topology-optimizable cross-section 80 mm x 80 mm nominal, 6 mm wall, ramping to a 180 mm diameter top flange 12 mm thick carrying the LiDAR mount: eight M8 (9 mm clearance) on a 160 mm bolt circle matching VLS-128 bottom pattern, plus a central 50 mm pass-through for the LiDAR data cable harness. Vertical offset from base flange plane to LiDAR mount plane 150 mm. Overall bounding envelope 220 x 200 x 170 mm. Material AlSi10Mg as-printed + T6: E 75 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2670 kg/m^3, yield 250 MPa, UTS 430 MPa at 20 C, endurance limit 97 MPa at 5e6 cycles per Renishaw / EOS material datasheet for LPBF AlSi10Mg. Environment: SAE J1455 Class III vibration (on-road automotive), -40 C to +85 C operating, outdoor rain/salt spray. Three load cases. Load Case 1 (road durability SAE J1455): 20-2000 Hz random vibration at 0.5 g_rms combined with LiDAR self-weight 13.5 kg; evaluate as 3 g sine peak at worst-case resonance plus static weight. Load Case 2 (highway side wind): at 130 km/h (36.1 m/s) vehicle speed, 0.62 kPa dynamic pressure on the 0.024 m^2 LiDAR projected frontal area produces 14.9 N drag + 30 N-m moment about the bracket base (150 mm moment arm); gust factor 2.0 per SAE J1455 gives 60 N-m moment design load. Load Case 3 (crash): 20g longitudinal deceleration on LiDAR mass = 2.65 kN horizontal pulse (200 ms half-sine) at the LiDAR CG 95 mm above the top flange (total moment arm 245 mm = 0.245 m), producing 650 N-m moment at the base. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 vibration, peak von Mises must not exceed 83 MPa (yield/3.0 to guarantee infinite life given 5e6 cycle exposure over vehicle life); (R2) under LC2 wind, peak stress must not exceed 125 MPa (yield/2.0 nominal road durability); (R3) under LC3 crash, peak stress 167 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R4) first six natural frequencies of the bracket + LiDAR (13.5 kg lumped at centroid 245 mm above base) must exceed 80 Hz to keep the LiDAR outside the SAE J1455 0-80 Hz vibration peak band; (R5) maximum tip displacement at the LiDAR mount plane under LC2 wind must not exceed 0.5 mm (keeps LiDAR boresight within point-cloud accuracy budget); (R6) mass must not exceed 1.5 kg as an additive-manufactured topology-optimized target (baseline bulk block = 220x200x170x2670e-9 = 19.9 kg, so target represents a 92% mass reduction via AM topology optimization); (R7) part must be self-supporting printable in a 45-degree overhang rule (AM constraint, checked by build-orientation angular rule). Thermal and radiation analyses are not required; fluid analysis reduced to equivalent static wind pressure. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic AlSi10Mg LPBF additively manufactured roof-mount bracket for a 360-degree mechanical LiDAR (e.g., Velodyne VLS-128, 13.5 kg) on a 2024 Chevrolet Blazer EV for the SAE AutoDrive Challenge II Year 4 Level-4 demonstrator. The bracket is a single 3D-printed part with an integral rear-rail base flange, a topology-optimizable vertical riser, and a top flange carrying the LiDAR bolt circle with a central cable pass-through. Design per SAE J1455 Class III vibration (-40 C to +85 C, outdoor), with the usual yield/2.0 durability and yield/1.5 transient safety factors and yield/3.0 on the vibration case for infinite-life intent.

Envelope: 220 x 200 x 170 mm with 150 mm vertical offset from base to LiDAR mount.

Load cases.
- LC1 road durability (SAE J1455): 20-2000 Hz random at 0.5 g_rms, evaluate as 3 g sine peak at worst-case resonance plus 13.5 kg static weight.
- LC2 highway side wind: 60 N-m design moment at the bracket base (130 km/h, 0.024 m^2 projected, gust factor 2.0 on 150 mm arm).
- LC3 crash: 20 g longitudinal on the LiDAR mass = 2.65 kN at CG 245 mm above base -> 650 N-m at the base.
Also include a 45-degree self-supporting-printability constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Mass target R6 (1.5 kg, 92% mass reduction vs bulk block) is practically unreachable without topology optimization outer loop calling the structural FEA.

prompt.title: AM AlSi10Mg LiDAR roof mount for AutoDrive Challenge II
prompt.brief: LPBF AlSi10Mg roof-rail mount for VLS-128 (13.5 kg) on Chevy Blazer EV; SAE J1455 Class III; 80 Hz modal floor; topology-optimized 1.5 kg.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: LiDAR thermal handled by sensor itself; bracket below T6 overage.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Not a buckling-critical geometry at these loads.

eval_coverage: SAE J1455 infinite-life vibration stress allowable
LiDAR boresight displacement at road-wind load
